name: Junaid Farooq
tagline: Building secure, resilient, and intelligent networks for the next generation.
bio: Junaid Farooq is an Assistant Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at the University of Michigan-Dearborn. His research focuses on wireless communications, NextG networks, cybersecurity, cyber-physical systems, and resilient networked systems. He leads the CYber REsilient Networks (CYREN) Lab, which develops innovative solutions for secure and resilient wireless networks, automated cybersecurity compliance, cyber risk management, and next-generation network architectures.

education.institution: King Abdullah University of Science & Technology (KAUST)

researchAreas.title: NextG & Wireless Networks
researchAreas.blurb: Architectures and algorithms for 5G/6G connectivity, spectrum efficiency, and self-organizing wireless infrastructure that adapts to demand in real time.

researchAreas.title: Cyber Resilience & Zero Trust
researchAreas.blurb: Proactive, automated security for untrusted infrastructure. Zero Trust principles, cyber risk quantification, and compliance for mission-critical systems.

researchAreas.title: IoT & Cyber-Physical Systems
researchAreas.blurb: Resource management and security for large-scale IoT deployments, supply chain risk, and the physical systems that depend on them.

researchAreas.title: Autonomous & Aerial Systems
researchAreas.blurb: Distributed orchestration of UAV networks for coverage, connectivity, and resilient service delivery in dynamic and contested environments.
